highlights.title: Opt-in Swift oref0 algorithm
changes: Adds an opt-in native Swift port of Trio's oref0 dosing algorithm (IOB, Meal, Autosens, DetermineBasal).
Adds a Settings toggle at Algorithm → Additionals → Use Swift Oref to enable the feature.
When enabled, Trio uses the Swift implementation for dosing instead of the bundled JavaScript runtime.
A green 'Swift Oref' badge is shown in the Loop Status view while the Swift path is active.
The JavaScript path remains the default and is unchanged unless you opt in.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1141: Port oref0 Algorithm from Javascript to Swift

highlights.title: Clamp very-low CGM readings to 39 mg/dL
changes: CGM readings below 39 mg/dL are raised to 39 before storage or use.
Backfilled CGM data is clamped the same way.
The original raw low reading and timestamp are kept in debug logs for diagnostics.
User-entered fingerstick (manual) glucose values are not altered.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1159: Fix: Floor CGM glucose at 39 mg/dL in GlucoseStorage

highlights.title: Glucose fetch follows meal absorption time
changes: Glucose readings used for meal/COB now cover your Max Meal Absorption Time plus 30 minutes.
The previous hard 6-hour cap on glucose history is removed so longer MMAT settings are respected.
Meal/COB should no longer spike above entered carbs or cliff-drop when MMAT exceeds 6 hours.
This behavior is applied consistently for both JS-based and native oref implementations.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1171: Change fetchLimit for glucose oref input to be dynamic based on maxMealAbsorptionTime #920

highlights.title: dynISF disabled only for high temporary targets
changes: Dynamic sensitivity adjustments (dynISF) will now activate for users whose profile target is above 118.
dynISF is only turned off when a temporary target is set at or above the half-basal exercise target (default 160).

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1189: Change profile target dynISF condition with HighTTRaisesSensitivity

highlights.title: Omnipod driver and Live Activity fixes
changes: Legacy OmniKit and OmniBLE drivers were removed; OmnipodKit is now the only Omnipod option.
Live Activity now shows active temp targets as a green band and fixes indefinite overrides and widget labels.
Watch app recovers from long staleness and refreshes quickly instead of getting stuck.
Today's Meals stats now show carbs, fat, and protein correctly instead of zeros.

items.title: Sync v0.8.2 patch to dev
changes: The development branch was updated to include the v0.8.2 patch release.
This merge synchronizes release changes back into the dev code base.
No specific user-facing changes are described in this merge.

sources.title: nightscout/Trio#1213: Trio v0.8.2 patch release – sync to dev
Merge pull request #1213 from nightscout/release/0.8.2 [skip ci]

includedPullRequests.title: Port oref0 Algorithm from Javascript to Swift
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary

Native Swift port of Trio's [JS-based oref0 algorithm](https://github.com/nightscout/trio-oref), replacing the JavaScript bridge with a first-class Swift implementation across all core algorithm modules: IOB, Meal, Autosens, and DetermineBasal (including Dynamic ISF).

### Background
Trio has historically executed its dosing algorithm by calling into a bundled JavaScript runtime (`trio-oref`). This port replaces that execution path end-to-end in Swift, removing the JS bridge dependency for users who — currently! — opt in. The port was developed between February 2025 and April 2026.

More details to be added at later point. The research paper produced in connection with this port will be provided at a later date.

###  Verification
Parity between the JavaScript and Swift implementations was verified through:

* **Mechanistic in silico simulation**: structural comparison of algorithm logic
* **Shadow execution replay**: 736,480 real-world algorithm invocations were replayed through both implementations and their outputs compared, covering all Swift modules

Notable behaviours verified include: IoB with long pump suspends, zombie carb detection, `currentDeviation` null handling in meal, `avgDelta` and `getGlucoseStatus` rounding, Dynamic ISF `tddRatio` / `insulinFactor` / `sensitivityRatio` outputs, and `expectedDelta` edge cases.

### Enabling

This is **opt-in**. The JavaScript path remains the default and is unchanged.

**Settings → Algorithm → Additionals → Use Swift Oref**

- **Off** (default): algorithm runs entirely in JavaScript, JS outputs drive insulin dosing
- **On**: algorithm runs entirely in Swift, Swift outputs drive insulin dosing

When enabled, a green `Swift Oref` badge appears in the Loop Status view to make it clear which path is active.

### Publication relating to this port 
Screven, T., Zhu, Z., Cengiz, D., Lal, R. A., Hood, K. K., & King, S. T. (2026). [*A Principled Framework for Safe Algorithm Updates in Automated Insulin Delivery Systems*](https://arxiv.org/html/2606.13882v1). arXiv:2606.13882v1.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix: Floor CGM glucose at 39 mg/dL in GlucoseStorage
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary

Addresses nightscout/Trio-dev#631 — since LibreTransmitter ignores Abbott given limits and propagates raw glucose, oref can receive values below the official 39 mg/dL CGM floor (LibreTransmitter explicitly bypasses the Libre algorithm's floor, forwarding values down to 1 mg/dL). On dev those sub-39 readings are silently dropped by the JS oref glucose-get-last filter (>38), leaving determination with no fresh BG and the pump stuck in whatever temp it was last on. The reporter saw ~1h 7min of no Swift determinations with a BG nadir of 23 mg/dL.

This PR adds a defensive chokepoint in BaseGlucoseStorage:

  - New Config.minimumGlucose = 39 and a private clampToMinimum(_:) helper that clamps both glucose and sgv on each
  BloodGlucose, emitting debug(.deviceManager, ...) with the raw value and timestamp so the original reading is preserved
  in support logs.
  - storeGlucose and backfillGlucose both run their input through clampToMinimum before any filtering or persistence.
  storeCGMState receives the clamped array too.
  - addManualGlucose is intentionally left untouched — user-entered fingerstick values are not silently rewritten.

  The floor aligns with BloodGlucose.isStateValid (already sgv >= 39) and FetchGlucoseManager.minimumSmoothedGlucose: 39.

includedPullRequests.title: Fix: Floor CGM glucose at 39 mg/dL in GlucoseStorage
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary

Addresses nightscout/Trio-dev#631 — since LibreTransmitter ignores Abbott given limits and propagates raw glucose, oref can receive values below the official 39 mg/dL CGM floor (LibreTransmitter explicitly bypasses the Libre algorithm's floor, forwarding values down to 1 mg/dL). On dev those sub-39 readings are silently dropped by the JS oref glucose-get-last filter (>38), leaving determination with no fresh BG and the pump stuck in whatever temp it was last on. The reporter saw ~1h 7min of no Swift determinations with a BG nadir of 23 mg/dL.

This PR adds a defensive chokepoint in BaseGlucoseStorage:

  - New Config.minimumGlucose = 39 and a private clampToMinimum(_:) helper that clamps both glucose and sgv on each
  BloodGlucose, emitting debug(.deviceManager, ...) with the raw value and timestamp so the original reading is preserved
  in support logs.
  - storeGlucose and backfillGlucose both run their input through clampToMinimum before any filtering or persistence.
  storeCGMState receives the clamped array too.
  - addManualGlucose is intentionally left untouched — user-entered fingerstick values are not silently rewritten.

  The floor aligns with BloodGlucose.isStateValid (already sgv >= 39) and FetchGlucoseManager.minimumSmoothedGlucose: 39.

includedPullRequests.title: Change fetchLimit for glucose oref input to be dynamic based on maxMealAbsorptionTime #920
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary
The glucose history fed into the meal/COB algorithm was capped at a fixed fetchLimit: 72 (72 readings × 5 min = 6h).
That constant predates the user-selectable maxMealAbsorptionTime (MMAT) setting, which ranges 4–10h. When MMAT exceeds 6h, the COB calculation considers carb absorption out to MMAT hours but only has 6h of glucose to work with.

The result is the bug reported in #920: mealCOB rises after a carb entry (to an amount larger than the carbs entered)
as larger absorption buckets slide out the back of the 6h window and are replaced by smaller front buckets, then crashes to zero when the meal is evicted at mealTime + MMAT. This drives over-bolusing and hypo risk.

This affects both the JS oref0 path and the native oref-swift port, since the limit is applied upstream in OpenAPS.determineBasal before either path runs.

The fix makes the fetch limit dynamic: ceil(maxMealAbsorptionTime × 12) readings, so the glucose window always covers the full absorption window. Preferences are loaded up front (the existing async load is just hoisted and reused) to derive the limit before the parallel fetches. Tying it to the setting rather than a static cap means it can't silently drift again if the MMAT range changes.

Credit to @robertjwellwood for the diagnosis and simulation in #920.

## To Test

  - Build and run with default MMAT (6h), then verify COB behaves as before
  - Set MMAT to 8–10h, enter a carb dose, and confirm COB decays smoothly toward 0 without rising above the entered amount or cliff-dropping
  - Verify both useSwiftOref on and off produce consistent COB trajectories (so JS-based oref and native Swift port)
  - Confirm glucoseFetchLimit scales as expected (4h→48, 6h→72, 10h→120)

includedPullRequests.title: Change fetchLimit for glucose oref input to be dynamic based on maxMealAbsorptionTime #920
includedPullRequests.body:
## Summary
The glucose history fed into the meal/COB algorithm was capped at a fixed fetchLimit: 72 (72 readings × 5 min = 6h).
That constant predates the user-selectable maxMealAbsorptionTime (MMAT) setting, which ranges 4–10h. When MMAT exceeds 6h, the COB calculation considers carb absorption out to MMAT hours but only has 6h of glucose to work with.

The result is the bug reported in #920: mealCOB rises after a carb entry (to an amount larger than the carbs entered)
as larger absorption buckets slide out the back of the 6h window and are replaced by smaller front buckets, then crashes to zero when the meal is evicted at mealTime + MMAT. This drives over-bolusing and hypo risk.

This affects both the JS oref0 path and the native oref-swift port, since the limit is applied upstream in OpenAPS.determineBasal before either path runs.

The fix makes the fetch limit dynamic: ceil(maxMealAbsorptionTime × 12) readings, so the glucose window always covers the full absorption window. Preferences are loaded up front (the existing async load is just hoisted and reused) to derive the limit before the parallel fetches. Tying it to the setting rather than a static cap means it can't silently drift again if the MMAT range changes.

Credit to @robertjwellwood for the diagnosis and simulation in #920.

## To Test

  - Build and run with default MMAT (6h), then verify COB behaves as before
  - Set MMAT to 8–10h, enter a carb dose, and confirm COB decays smoothly toward 0 without rising above the entered amount or cliff-dropping
  - Verify both useSwiftOref on and off produce consistent COB trajectories (so JS-based oref and native Swift port)
  - Confirm glucoseFetchLimit scales as expected (4h→48, 6h→72, 10h→120)

includedPullRequests.title: Change profile target dynISF condition with HighTTRaisesSensitivity
includedPullRequests.body:
~~- Before this change, condition checked whether highTTRaisesSens was active AND user's profile target >= 118~~
~~- We received an issue report by a parent whose child runs with profile target set at >118, thus dynISF NEVER activated~~
~~- Change now moves this check condition to a saner target of halfBasalExerciseTarget, to ONLY disable dynISF if the user ACTUALLY sets a TT at a target value >= HBT (which by default is 160)~~

Removes a legacy (pre Trio) dynISF condition that would disable dynISF.

includedPullRequests.title: Trio v0.8.2 patch release
includedPullRequests.body:
## What's Changed At A Glance

- **OmnipodKit is now the sole Omnipod driver** — OmniBLE and OmniKit submodules removed; "All Omnipod Types" is the only Omnipod entry in Add Pump and Onboarding
- **Live Activity Enhancements / Fixes:**
   -  Temp target on chart — active temp targets now render as a green band on the Live Activity chart, with a named badge alongside the override badge
   - Ondefinite override fix — overrides with no set duration no longer collapse the chart's rule mark to the current moment
   - COB label and widget string fixes — corrected the COB string and localized all widget item labels
- **Watch connectivity fix** — massively improve watch performance; stale data on watch now updates in just a moment vs. getting stuck during processing.
- **Fix Stats Meals showing zero macros for current day** — today's carbs, fat, and protein now always appear correctly in the Meals chart
---

## Full List of PRs in `0.8.2`

| PR | Title | Author |
|---|---|---|
| [#1180](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1180) | Add temp target display to Live Activity lock screen widget | [@t1dude](https://github.com/t1dude) |
| [#1182](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1182) | Update CODEOWNERS: add @marionbarker and @kingst | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |
| [#1184](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1184) | Fix COB string, localize Live Activity widget item labels | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |
| [#1188](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1188) | Fix Stats Meals showing zero macros for current day | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |
| [#1190](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1190) | Fix watch app back-sync after long staleness | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |
| [#1192](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1192) | Fix indefinite override not drawn in Live Activity chart | [@t1dude](https://github.com/t1dude) |
| [#1200](https://github.com/nightscout/Trio/pull/1200) | Remove Legacy Omnipod Submodules (OmniKit, OmniBLE) | [@dnzxy](https://github.com/dnzxy) |

---

## Contributors

@dnzxy @t1dude @marv-out

includedCommits.subject: Change profile target dynISF condition with HighTTRaisesSend - Before this change, condition checked whether highTTRaisesSens was active AND user's profile target >= 118 - We received an issue report by a parent whose child runs with profile target set at >118, thus dynISF NEVER activated - Change now moves this check condition to a saner target of halfBasalExerciseTarget, to ONLY disable dynISF if the user ACTUALLY sets a TT at a target value >= HBT (which by default is 160)
includedCommits.author: Deniz Cengiz
